input.background: The smoking prevalence example in this chapter $(n=57, x=17)$ estimated a population proportion of 0.30 with margin of error (assuming $95 \%$ confidence) equal to $\pm 0.12$.
input.question: How large a sample would be needed to derive an estimate with a margin of error of $\pm 0.05$?

output.method: 达到指定概率所需的最小样本量
output.answer: $n=\frac{z_1^2-p^* q^*}{m^2}=\frac{1.96^2 \cdot 0.30 \cdot 0.70}{0.05^2}=322.7$. Use a sample of 323 observations.

sample_size.description: The size of the sample needed to achieve the desired margin of error.

current_sample_size.description: The size of the sample used in the previous estimate.

current_margin_of_error.description: The margin of error in the previous estimate.

desired_margin_of_error.description: The desired margin of error for the new estimate.

confidence_level.description: The confidence level used to calculate the margin of error.

estimated_proportion.description: The estimated population proportion from the previous sample.
